revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cs:Klan Amago]][[en:Amago clan]][[fa:خاندان آماگو]][[fr:Clan Amago]][[it:Clan Amago]][[ja:尼子氏]][[pt:Clã Amago]][[ru:Амаго (род)]][[uk:Рід Амаґо]][[zh:尼子氏]]
{{Short description|Japanese Clan|qq=}} {{Infobox Japanese clan |surname = Amago |surname nihongo = 尼子 |image = File:Kamon yotumeyui.png |image size = 140px |image caption = The emblem ([[:en:Mon (badge)|''mon'']]) of the Amago clan |home province ={{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|qid=Q582846|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo Province}} |parent house ={{ill|Sasaki clan|en|Sasaki clan|qid=Q1028587|lt=Sasaki clan|1=Sasaki clan}} |titles =Various |founder =Sasaki Takahisa |final ruler ={{ill|Amago Yoshihisa|en|Amago Yoshihisa|qid=Q837428|lt=Amago Yoshihisa|1=Amago Yoshihisa}} |current head = |founding year =14th century |dissolution = |ruled until =1566, Fall of {{ill|Gassantoda Castle|en|Gassantoda Castle|qid=Q1039004|lt=Gassantoda Castle|1=Gassantoda Castle}} |cadet branches = }} The {{nihongo|'''Amago clan'''|尼子氏|Amago-shi|qq=}}, descended from the {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|qid=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda|1=Emperor Uda}} (868–897) by the [[:en:Kyogoku clan|Kyogoku clan]], descending from the {{ill|Sasaki clan|en|Sasaki clan|qid=Q1028587|lt=Sasaki clan|1=Sasaki clan}} ({{ill|Uda Genji|en|Uda Genji|qid=Q4846201|lt=Uda Genji|1=Uda Genji}}). Kyogoku Takahisa in the 14th century, lived in Amako-go (Izumo Province), and took the name 'Amago'.<ref name=kotobank>{{cite web|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%BC%E5%AD%90%E6%B0%8F-27143|title=Amago clan|publisher=kotobank|access-date=22 October 2021|qq=}}</ref> The family crest is also the one of the Kyogoku clan. They were Shugodai (vice-Governors) of Izumo and Oki provinces for generations, for the Kyogoku Shugo branch, and their seat was Gassan Toda castle. In 1484, Amago Tsunehisa (1458–1541), was deprived of the position of Shugodai by Kyogoku Masatsune, who was the Shugo, because he did not obey the request of tax from the Muromachi bakufu, and was expelled from Gassan Toda castle. Although Enya Kamonnosuke was dispatched to Gassan Toda castle as the new Shugodai, Tsunehisa recaptured Gassan Toda castle by a surprise attack in 1486, took control of Izumo, and developed the Amago clan into a Sengoku Daimyo clan. The Amago fought the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|en|Ōuchi clan|qid=Q1348909|lt=Ōuchi clan|1=Ōuchi clan}} or the {{ill|Mōri clan|en|Mōri clan|qid=Q914637|lt=Mōri clan|1=Mōri clan}} (who had been among their vassals), during Japan's {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}.<ref name=kotobank/> For much of the next hundred years, the clan battled with the Ōuchi and Mōri, who controlled neighboring provinces, and fell into decline when {{ill|Gassantoda Castle|en|Gassantoda Castle|qid=Q1039004|lt=Gassantoda Castle|1=Gassantoda Castle}} fell to the Mōri in 1566.<ref name=kotobank/> {{ill|Amago Katsuhisa|en|Amago Katsuhisa|qid=Q837431|lt=Amago Katsuhisa|1=Amago Katsuhisa}} tried to regain prestige for the clan by joining the forces of {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|qid=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, invaded {{ill|Tajima Province|en|Tajima Province|qid=Q1053727|lt=Tajima|1=Tajima Province}} and {{ill|Inaba Province|en|Inaba Province|qid=Q1054338|lt=Inaba|1=Inaba Province}} Provinces, but was defeated and died in the {{ill|Siege of Kōzuki Castle|en|Siege of Kōzuki Castle|qid=Q7510180|lt=siege of Kōzuki|1=Siege of Kōzuki Castle}} by the Mōri in 1578. ==Clan heads== # '''Amago Takahisa''' (尼子高久, 1363–1391) # '''Amago Mochihisa''' (尼子持久, 1381–1437) # '''Amago Kiyosada''' (尼子清定, 1410–1488) # '''{{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|en|Amago Tsunehisa|qid=Q1045252|lt=Amago Tsunehisa|1=Amago Tsunehisa}}''' (尼子経久, 1458–1541) # '''{{ill|Amago Haruhisa|en|Amago Haruhisa|qid=Q3273106|lt=Amago Haruhisa|1=Amago Haruhisa}}''' (尼子晴久, 1514–1561) # '''{{ill|Amago Yoshihisa|en|Amago Yoshihisa|qid=Q837428|lt=Amago Yoshihisa|1=Amago Yoshihisa}}''' (尼子義久, 1540–1610) # '''Amago Motosato''' (尼子元知, 1572 or 1598–1622) # '''Amago Nariyasu''' (尼子就易, ? –1659) # '''Amago Ujihisa''' (尼子氏久, 1646–1710) # '''Amago Motouji''' (尼子元氏, ? –1730) # '''Amago Tokihisa''' (尼子時久, 1690–1752) # '''Amago Narikiyo''' (尼子就清, 1721–1788) # '''Amago Fusataka''' (尼子房高, 1754–1827) # '''Amago Motohisa''' (尼子元久) # '''Amago Chikanobu''' (尼子親辰) # '''Amago Michisuke''' (尼子道介) # '''Amago Shigehisa''' (尼子薫久) # '''Amago Torasuke''' (尼子寅介) ==Notable members== * [[Amago Kiyosada]] (d. 1487) * {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|en|Amago Tsunehisa|qid=Q1045252|lt=Amago Tsunehisa|1=Amago Tsunehisa}} (1458–1541) * [[Amago Hisayuki]] (d. 1541) * [[Amago Masahisa]] (1488–1513) * {{ill|Amago Okihisa|en|Amago Okihisa|qid=Q4739137|lt=Amago Okihisa|1=Amago Okihisa}} (1497–1534) * {{ill|Amago Haruhisa|en|Amago Haruhisa|qid=Q3273106|lt=Amago Haruhisa|1=Amago Haruhisa}} (1514–1562) * {{ill|Amago Kunihisa|en|Amago Kunihisa|qid=Q4739136|lt=Amago Kunihisa|1=Amago Kunihisa}} (d. 1554) * [[Amago Masahisa]] (d. 1554) * {{ill|Amago Katsuhisa|en|Amago Katsuhisa|qid=Q837431|lt=Amago Katsuhisa|1=Amago Katsuhisa}} (1553–1578) * {{ill|Amago Yoshihisa|en|Amago Yoshihisa|qid=Q837428|lt=Amago Yoshihisa|1=Amago Yoshihisa}} (1540–1610) ==Retainers and vassals== The Amago's chief generals were called ''Amago 10 Yushi'' (尼子十勇士). * {{ill|Yamanaka Yukimori|en|Yamanaka Yukimori|qid=Q8047875|lt=Yamanaka Yukimori|1=Yamanaka Yukimori}} (1544–1578) * [[Akiage Iorinosuke]] * [[Yokoji Hyōgonosuke]] (d. 1570) * [[Ueda Saenosuke]] * [[Teramoto Seishinosuke]] * [[Motomo Dorinosuke]] * [[Kogura Nezuminosuke]] * [[Fukada Doronosuke]] * [[Hayakawa Ayunosuke]] * [[Yabunaka Ibaranosuke]] Other vassals: * {{ill|Uyama Hisakane|en|Uyama Hisakane|qid=Q7904093|lt=Uyama Hisakane|1=Uyama Hisakane}} * [[Tachihara Hisatsuna]] * [[Ushio Yoshikiyo]] ==Amago JiCategoryi== Amago clan's 10 castles (Defense network and 10 supporting castles of {{ill|Gassantoda Castle|en|Gassantoda Castle|qid=Q1039004|lt=Gassantoda Castle|1=Gassantoda Castle}} called '''Amago JiCategoryi'''.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sanin-chuo.co.jp/articles/-/47566|title=尼子の山城 魅力化へ結集 出雲斐川６０人 顕彰会発足|date=13 June 2021 |publisher=The San-in Chuo Shimpo|access-date=22 October 2021|qq=}}</ref>) * [[Shiraga Castle]] (白鹿城) * [[Mitoya Castle]] (三刀屋城) * Mizawa Castle (三沢城) * Akana Castle (赤穴城) * [[Ushio Catle]] (牛尾城) * Takase Castle (高瀬城) * Jinzai Castle (神西城) * [[Kumano Castle]] (熊野城) * Daisai Castle (大西城) * Maki Castle (真木城) ===Gallery=== <gallery class="center" widths="150px" heights="150px"> Shiraga castle.jpg|Shiraga Castle Mitoya Castle.jpg|Mitoya Castle Akana Castle.jpg|Akana Castle Kumano Castle.jpg|Kumano Castle Ushio castle.jpg|Ushio Castle Takase Castle.jpg|Takase Castle Daisai castle.jpg|Daisai Castle Mizawajouoote01.jpg|Stone wall of Mizawa Castle </gallery> == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} ==References== *Frederic, Louis (2002). "Japan Encyclopedia." Cambridge, Massachusetts: Harvard University Press. {{Japan-clan-stub|qq=}}
[[en:Amago clan]]
